mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, which historically correlates with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: INDPRO's 20-day forward return correlation does not exceed 0.2 over any 3-year window; or the 95% bootstrap confidence interval of rank IC includes zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5%, it signals labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) in periods where UNRATE>5%, or the average return is greater than -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in housing starts and rise in unemployment jointly signal severe demand contraction, which depresses equity valuations over a quarterly horizon as recession risks intensify.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines over 3 months and UNRATE rises over 3 months
failure_condition: In periods with joint HOUST decline and UNRATE rise, the average 60-day forward return is not lower than the unconditional average by a statistically significant margin (p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales are a positive signal for equity returns only when the housing market is robust (housing starts above 1.5 million annualized). In early 2008, with starts at 1.187 million, this signal is inactive, protecting against false positives from temporary retail strength.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST threshold derived from pre-2005 boom years
failure_condition: When the HOUST>1500 condition holds, the average 5-day forward return after rising RSAFS (above 1-month average) is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive two-month decline in industrial production signals a manufacturing recession that erodes earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: In months following a 2-month decline in INDPRO, the average 5-day forward return is not negative or not below -0.3% with 90% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: A steady increase in total nonfarm payroll employment reflects labor market strength that boosts consumer confidence and spending, supporting corporate profits and causing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between PAYEMS growth rate and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is below 0.1 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation and unemployment are simultaneously rising (stagflation), real purchasing power and corporate margins contract, while the central bank faces a policy dilemma, resulting in negative equity returns over the forecast horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where both proxies rise above thresholds, 20-day forward returns are not more negative than returns in other periods at a 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales growth exceeds 5% year-over-year, it signals robust consumer demand that quickly lifts equity prices, but the effect is transitory and returns may mean-revert within a quarter.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following RSAFS YoY growth >5% is not significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.20).
